name: Ambulatory & Outpatient Facility Cleaning
centralRisk: terminal clean between cases, under schedule pressure
regulatoryFramework: 105 CMR 140 — Massachusetts DPH, Licensure of Clinics
CDC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control in Health-Care Facilities
h1Pattern: Ambulatory & Outpatient Facility Cleaning in {city}, MA

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Operating rooms not cleaned and disinfected with an EPA-registered disinfectant after each surgical or invasive procedure. On the CMS surveyor worksheet a 'no' here must be cited as a deficient practice.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CMS Exhibit 351, ASC Infection Control Surveyor Worksheet, Section IV item A — cited against 42 CFR 416.51(a)

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Operating rooms not terminally cleaned daily.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CMS Exhibit 351, Section IV item B — cited against 42 CFR 416.51(a)

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Environmental surfaces in patient care areas not cleaned and disinfected on a regular basis, when spills occur, and when visibly contaminated.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CMS Exhibit 351, Section IV item C — cited against 42 CFR 416.51(a)

commonSurveyFindings.finding: No procedure in place to decontaminate gross spills of blood.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CMS Exhibit 351, Section IV item D — cited against 42 CFR 416.51(a)

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Cleaning staff not included in the centre's documented infection control training. The surveyor worksheet names cleaning staff as a category and asks for documentation proving they were trained.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CMS Exhibit 351, questions 19, 19b, 19d and 19e — related to 42 CFR 416.51(b)

commonSurveyFindings.finding: The centre cannot document that it considered, selected and implemented nationally recognised infection control guidelines covering environmental services. The infection control programme is a Condition for Coverage, so this can draw a condition-level citation.
Failure to provide a functional and sanitary environment for surgical services — the umbrella finding every environmental cleaning deficiency in a surgery centre is cited under.
Reprocessing area without a one-directional workflow from contaminated to clean and sterile areas — no clear separation between soiled and clean workspaces.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CDC Infection Prevention Checklist for Outpatient Settings, Section II item C

_publishGate: Requires >=10 faqs and >=6 commonSurveyFindings before this service may go live. Survey findings are real regulatory citations — never invented, never inferred from the regulation text. Source: data/_todo-clinical-input.md.
